div.head: The Chargé at Tangier (Childs) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
132. Legation’s 120, May 16.Not printed. Orgaz has now replied to Legation’s note of May 18 requesting expulsion of German agents from Spanish North Africa (see paragraph 6, section 1, Madrid’s 2430, May 4Printed in vol. iv , first section under Spain.). He states he likewise is interested in execution of Madrid agreement and that necessary measures have been taken for departure from zone of Germans in question. He has ordered brief investigations of these
individuals to “guarantee strict equity and justice.” As soon as necessary investigations are completed he will inform Legation of their departure dates and respective routes. Department should note that foregoing refers to German agents in Spanish Morocco and Tangier as distinguished from German consular personnel in Tangier. Arrangements for departure of last named are now in progress.
Repeated to Algiers as our 38, repeated to Madrid as our 60.
